records.ety: [LL. complacentia: cf. F. complaisance. See Complacent, and cf. Complaisance.]

quotes: The inward complacence we find in acting reasonably and virtuously.
Others proclaim the infirmities of a great man with satisfaction and complacency, if they discover none of the like in themselves.

defs: The cause of pleasure or joy.
The manifestation of contentment or satisfaction; good nature; kindness; civility; affability.
quotes: Complacency, and truth, and manly sweetness, Dwell ever on his tongue, and smooth his thoughts.
With mean complacence ne'er betray your trust.
